column1: # Workflow WRITER filesystem policy (A3 — bounded journal I/O off the VM thread).
# Every `write_all` / `fs::write` in the sema-workflow crate MUST live on the per-run
# journal WRITER thread (writer.rs). The VM-thread `Journal` methods (journal.rs) only
# render + `try_send`; journal.rs keeps ONLY the run-dir open/claim (create_new /
# create_dir), never a synchronous write. This pins the writes to writer.rs at exact
# counts; a `write_all`/`fs::write` in ANY other sema-workflow file (a regressed synchronous
# `Journal::write`) is unallowlisted and fails. The scan comment-strips and counts the
# production module only (the #[cfg(test)] block is never shipped).

column4: events.jsonl line + the journal-size-cap overflow marker, on the writer thread
memo sidecar + whole-file sidecar (args/metadata/result), on the writer thread
